affected_outputs: librarian novelty verdict|benchmark interpretation|final scientific claims
description: The broad class of classically conformal dark U(1) phase transitions has already been studied; novelty is only partial for the exact no-fermion, charge-one, PTArcade implementation.
required_solution: Frame novelty narrowly and compare against the closest U(1) Coleman-Weinberg/PTA papers with explicit model-content differences.
source_note: Not novel as a broad class; partially novel only in the stripped-down convention and implementation.

description: Literature benchmarks use multiple U(1) charge and vev conventions, including m_ZD = 2 g_D M, while this pipeline uses q_Phi = 1 and m_Aprime = g_D v.
required_solution: Maintain an explicit conversion table for each imported benchmark and verify all mass, vev, and coupling translations before quoting comparisons.
source_note: Closest same-model paper uses a different dark charge/mass convention.

description: lambda_Phi is fixed by the Coleman-Weinberg/backend relation and is not an independent scanned parameter.
required_solution: Keep lambda_Phi out of independent priors and record beta_lambda/lambda_Phi as derived quantities for every posterior sample.
source_note: The critic and FOPT handoffs consistently treat lambda_Phi as dependent.

description: epsilon and lambda_HP are unspecified and assumed negligible for FOPT, but they are essential for laboratory, cosmological, and visible-sector constraints.
required_solution: Specify priors or benchmark values for epsilon and lambda_HP and implement the corresponding width, lifetime, branching-ratio, and production calculations.
source_note: No collider, beam-dump, SN1987A, BBN, CMB, direct, or indirect exclusion was applied without these observables.

description: The FOPT/PTA calculation fixes xi = T_dark/T_visible = 1 while the couplings that could maintain thermal contact are small or unspecified.
required_solution: Compute Gamma_SM-dark/H, decoupling, entropy transfer, and xi(T), or explicitly impose and justify xi = 1 as an initial condition.
source_note: xi=1 is an input assumption, not a derived result.

description: The FOPT stage uses the semi-analytic conformal single-field backend with a high-temperature/small-field polynomial form and subleading scalar/Goldstone treatment.
required_solution: Benchmark against a full finite-temperature effective-potential implementation over representative posterior points.
source_note: Semi-analytic backend with scalar/Goldstone contributions treated as subleading.

description: Full Daisy resummation, RG running, longitudinal gauge thermal masses, scalar thermal masses, and gauge/resummation prescription variations were not implemented.
required_solution: Add a controlled finite-temperature potential implementation and compare Landau-gauge, gauge-independent, and resummation-scheme variants where available.
source_note: Not required by the selected backend, but required for a full finite-temperature implementation.

description: The backend uses a Gaussian false-vacuum-fraction/percolation approximation; percolation and eternal-inflation checks are present only at backend-screen level.
required_solution: Run posterior-level percolation and false-vacuum survival checks with uncertainty bands for the preferred region.
source_note: Percolation_checked is true, but the method remains approximate.

description: The PTA-preferred points involve very large alpha, sometimes extremely large, so standard beta/H, reheating, and fluid approximations may not be reliably controlled.
required_solution: Audit posterior samples for supercooling validity, reheating consistency, vacuum domination, and beta/H definition robustness.
source_note: Best 14/14 benchmark has alpha about 2.1e6; another has alpha about 2.1e11.

description: Wall velocity, microscopic friction, runaway conditions, and the bulk-flow/dissipative-bulk-flow partition were not computed.
required_solution: Implement a wall-dynamics module or scan conservative wall/friction regimes and propagate the uncertainty through the PTA spectrum.
source_note: bf was selected because dissipative wall-friction inputs are absent.

description: Published finite-temperature comparisons and related benchmarks often use Landau-gauge one-loop potentials; gauge dependence remains an uncontrolled theory systematic.
required_solution: Compare against gauge-independent approximations or vary gauge/resummation prescriptions to estimate a theory envelope.
source_note: Gauge dependence of finite-temperature effective potentials is flagged across relevant papers.

description: The bf spectrum template is the closest implemented template for a strongly supercooled transition, not a first-principles validation of this model's GW source.
required_solution: Propagate template alternatives and wall-dynamics uncertainty, and avoid interpreting the bf posterior as uniquely model-selected.
source_note: Closest-template choice with caveats.

description: The higgsless sound-wave template was considered but not selected because the large-alpha supercooled regime is outside clean sound-wave validity assumptions.
required_solution: Keep the sound-wave score as a diagnostic only unless a validity calculation supports it.
source_note: higgsless scored bins but was not selected for physical validity reasons.

description: PTArcade completed one 100000-sample campaign with finite chains, but no multi-chain convergence, autocorrelation, effective-sample-size, or prior-sensitivity study was performed.
required_solution: Run independent chains, inspect autocorrelation and effective sample size, and repeat with alternative priors/templates.
source_note: Acceptance and finite rows were checked, but convergence was not.

description: PTArcade samples v through a log-uniform prior on log10_v over 0.003-5 GeV and g_D through a uniform prior over 0.42-1.05; these choices are informative.
required_solution: Run prior-range and measure-sensitivity checks, including wider/narrower v and g_D ranges and explicit reporting of boundary posterior mass.
source_note: v prior is uniform in log10_v; g_D prior is uniform linearly.

description: The FOPT seed scan covered v = 0.01-1.0 GeV and g_D = 0.1-0.9, while the PTA/PTArcade prior later expanded to v = 0.003-5.0 GeV and g_D = 0.42-1.05.
required_solution: Document why the later PTA range is valid and run a consistent posterior-driven FOPT diagnostic over the full PTArcade prior box.
source_note: PTA stage expanded the explored range around viable benchmarks.

description: f_peak_est in the FOPT scan is only an order-of-magnitude redshifted frequency scale setter and not the physical PTA template peak.
required_solution: Continue using full PTA spectra for likelihood work and keep f_peak_est out of final spectral claims.
source_note: The handoff explicitly warns that PTA spectral templates belong to the PTA stage.

affected_outputs: phenomenological viability|allowed parameter space|final exclusion statements
description: Most experimental, astrophysical, and cosmological constraints were not applied because required observables were missing.
required_solution: Treat the current result as unconstrained by those searches until the missing observables and recasts are implemented.
source_note: Only 2 constraints were directly testable; 11 require a new backend.

description: A_prime widths, visible/invisible branching fractions, decay lengths, production rates, and recasts are missing because epsilon and decay assumptions are absent.
required_solution: Implement dark_photon_widths.py and dark_photon_recasts.py with an epsilon prior and decay-channel model.
source_note: Dark photon limits cannot be applied from mass alone.

affected_outputs: light scalar searches|Higgs exotic constraints|BBN/CMB scalar limits
description: rho decay widths, Higgs mixing, portal production, and scalar lifetimes are missing; rho -> A_prime A_prime is closed in the PTArcade 1sigma box.
required_solution: Implement lambda_HP to scalar-Higgs mixing, rho partial widths, and cosmological lifetime/yield calculations.
source_note: Visible rho decays are portal controlled.

description: BBN, CMB, Delta N_eff, freeze-in, residual abundance, and late energy-injection constraints are not computed.
required_solution: Implement thermal_history.py, cosmology_constraints.py, and neff.py with portal couplings, decay tables, yields, and entropy tracking.
source_note: Masses and xi=1 are insufficient to apply cosmology bounds.

description: The constraints agent treated the PTArcade 1sigma region as a rectangular box rather than reconstructing correlated posterior samples.
required_solution: Propagate actual chain samples through derived masses and future constraint backends.
source_note: Parameter correlations were not reconstructed from the chain.

description: Direct-detection constraints are not applicable to the validated minimal no-fermion model because no stable dark matter particle or local density fraction is defined.
required_solution: Do not quote direct-detection bounds unless a stable dark matter candidate is added.
source_note: Direct detection can be reopened only if a DM candidate is added.

description: Closest PTA-scale conformal U(1) benchmarks include additional fermions, dark matter dynamics, or different thermal histories that can shift thermal masses and viable priors.
required_solution: Separate same-model, structurally similar, and target-signal-only benchmarks in all reports and avoid transferring priors without caveats.
source_note: arXiv:2502.19478 is close but includes anomaly-cancelling fermions and dark matter dynamics.

description: The FOPT f_peak_est uses g_* interpolated from a table and clamped at table boundaries.
required_solution: For final spectra, use the PTA template computation; if f_peak_est is reported, include the g_* interpolation/clamping caveat.
source_note: g_* for f_peak_est is interpolated and clamped.

affected_outputs: Bayesian model comparison|strength of PTA preference claims
description: The PTArcade handoff reports posterior summaries but no evidence estimate or evidence error, so model comparison support is absent.
required_solution: Run an evidence-capable campaign or restrict claims to posterior fits under the chosen model/template/prior.
source_note: evidence and evidence_error are null.
